---
title: "tbox-finder — Phase 1 (Backbones & heads) dev-log"
author: "bioedca"
format:
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

<!-- Rendered with the Typst engine (quarto ≥1.9, `format: typst`): the
     laptop/CI toolchain ships no LaTeX, and Typst produces the PDF natively.
     Renders via `conda run -n tbox-viz quarto render analyses/phase1_log.qmd`. -->


# Phase 1 — Backbones & heads

Per-step development log (CLAUDE.md §2.4). One stanza is appended after every
step's commit; this file renders to `docs/dev-log/phase1_<YYYY-MM-DD>.pdf` at
the phase-exit gate.

## Step P1-01 — 2026-07-12: Stage-1 CUDA-kernel import/forward smoke gate + pure-PyTorch fallback cost

**Inputs.** The pinned GPU env **`tbox-ml-dna`** (`envs/ml-dna.yml` + `ml-dna.conda-lock.yml`:
torch 2.7.1+cu128, mamba-ssm 2.2.6.post3, causal-conv1d 1.6.2.post1, flash-attn 2.8.3.post1);
the P0-06 `sm_86` wheel-availability finding (ADR-0002 A2); a seeded random-DNA token fixture
(`tests/fixtures/kernel_smoke_dna/dna_tokens.npy`, seed 42, `[2,512]` int8).

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/kernels/kernel_smoke.py` (run via `slurm/p1/kernel_smoke.sbatch` on
one cluster RTX A4000, Ampere `sm_86`, driver 590.48.01) probes the runtime, asserts
`import selective_scan_cuda` (ADR-0002 A2 C2), embeds the DNA fixture into selective-scan inputs
(fp32; D=512, N=16, L=512, B=2), and forwards through both the CUDA kernels
(`selective_scan_fn`/`causal_conv1d_fn`) and the hand-wired pure-PyTorch references
(`selective_scan_ref`/`causal_conv1d_ref`), recording kernel-vs-ref max-abs parity and tokens/sec.
Kernel APIs were verified against the library source at the pinned tags (v2.2.6.post3 / v1.6.2.post1)
**and** the installed env (context7 quota-blocked). The first run (job 460) exposed a partial
`causal-conv1d` install in the cluster env (orphan `.so`, no package/dist-info) — repaired by
reinstalling the exact pinned wheel; the smoke was hardened to record import failures in-report
rather than crash (the job-460 crash left no report).

**Outputs.** `reports/p1/kernel_smoke.json` (git_sha `8ba3880`, SLURM job 462, `measured=true`);
`src/tbox_finder/kernels/{__init__,kernel_smoke}.py`; `slurm/p1/kernel_smoke.sbatch`;
`tests/unit/test_kernel_smoke_report.py` (14 cases, CPU-only); `tests/fixtures/kernel_smoke_{dna,sample}/`;
**ADR-0002 Amendment A5**.

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** (`gate.overall_pass=true`): import + forward green on an A4000 `sm_86`
(`selective_scan_cuda` + `causal_conv1d_cuda` + mamba-ssm/causal-conv1d/flash-attn all import);
`selective_scan` parity **1.53e-5** and `causal_conv1d` **9.5e-7** (both ≪ `atol` 1e-2); measured
pure-PyTorch `selective_scan` fallback throughput ratio **≈871×** (CUDA 12.46 M vs ref 14.3 K tok/s);
`causal_conv1d` **0.56×** (the D3 optional accelerator — small-tensor launch overhead, correctness is
what the gate needs). Artifact-based verification (`sacct` off): `squeue` empty + `kernel_smoke.DONE`
present + zero-byte `.err`.

**Decisions.** The "pure-PyTorch fallback" is `selective_scan_ref` invoked directly — the packaged
`Mamba` block never reaches it (ADR-0002 A2 C2), so the ≈871× slowdown quantitatively confirms the
prebuilt `sm_86` wheel is **load-bearing**, not a convenience. `atol` is an implementer choice (ADR-0002
pins no kernel-vs-fallback tolerance; the raw diff is always recorded, §10.3). Cross-link **ADR-0002 A5**.

**Commit.** `8ba3880` (scaffold `5f18180` + hardening `8ba3880`) + the follow-up carrying this stanza,
the report, and A5; squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in `TODO.md` at P1-01 close.

## Step P1-02 — 2026-07-12: Load Caduceus-PS default checkpoint + human-reference pretraining provenance

**Inputs.** The default Stage-1 checkpoint — Caduceus-PS
`kuleshov-group/caduceus-ps_seqlen-131k_d_model-256_n_layer-16` (7.73 M params, `d_model` 256,
16 MambaDNA layers, RC-equivariant) — pinned at the **immutable commit** `d89eeb85…` (never `main`);
the `tbox-ml-dna` env (transformers 4.57.5 `trust_remote_code` ceiling; ADR-0002 D2/A4); a short
seeded DNA fixture + its reverse-complement built in token space via the config `complement_map`.

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/models/caduceus_backbone.py::load_caduceus_ps` loads the backbone
(`AutoModel`, `trust_remote_code=True`) at the pinned revision on the **local GPU** (RTX 4060, `sm_89`),
counts parameters, and measures RC-equivariance — `f(RC(x))` vs `f(x).flip(dims=(-2,-1))` (the
model-card hidden-state RC transform) — with a reverse-only negative control. The CLI writes the
canonical `data/interim/caduceus_ps_131k/provenance.json` sidecar (`provenance.py`; ADR-0002 as the
pinning ADR). **imp.md↔ADR reconciliation:** imp.md's "pure-PyTorch CPU forward" is superseded by
ADR-0002 A2 C2 (the packaged `Mamba` hard-imports `selective_scan_cuda`; no CPU forward exists) —
resolved via the local GPU (still LOCAL, no SLURM). Loader/RC APIs verified against the installed
transformers 4.57.5 + the HF model card (context7 quota-blocked).

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/models/{__init__,caduceus_backbone}.py`; `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml`;
`data/interim/caduceus_ps_131k/provenance.json` (git-tracked, `measured=true`, git_sha `bd64aa6`);
`tests/unit/test_caduceus_backbone.py` (23 cases: 21 bare + 2 GPU). **No DVC artifact** (metadata
sidecar; weights are pulled from HF by revision, not stored).

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** (`gate.overall_pass=true`): loads at the pinned revision; **param count
== 7,725,312** (== `EXPECTED_PARAM_COUNT`, the PRD "7.73 M" to the exact integer); **RC-equivariance
max-abs-diff 0.0** ≤ `atol` 1e-4 (bit-exact), reverse-only negative control **2.39** (the check
discriminates); provenance carries `pretraining.domain = "human reference genome"` + ≥2 citations + the
`ml-dna` env-lock hash. Bare tier **21 passed / 2 skipped** (the CI path); GPU tier green under
`tbox-ml-dna` (§8.5 manual gate); `TBOX_REQUIRE_CADUCEUS=1` rot-guard fails closed; full unit suite
**396 passed / 53 skipped** (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean.

**Decisions.** The checkpoint is pinned by an **immutable commit SHA** (never `main`, ADR-0002 D2); the
param count + equivariance are **frozen in code** (no config can weaken them — the coverage.py
`OOD_ECE_MIN_N` precedent). RC-equivariance is asserted on the **hidden states** (what the P1-03 head
consumes) via the model-card `flip(dims=(-2,-1))` transform — the §6 strand-resolver precondition.
**Scientific-evidence gate (§10.1) CLEARED** for the load-bearing human-reference pretraining fact:
≥2 authoritative agreeing sources — HF model card (verbatim) + Schiff et al. (arXiv:2403.03234;
PMID:40567809), both already in-repo (ADR-0002). The gate is **hardware-agnostic** (`sm_89` laptop;
RC-equivariance is an architectural property of RCPS, identical on the cluster A4000 `sm_86`).
Cross-link **ADR-0002 D7**.

**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-02-caduceus` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-02 close); feeds **P1-03** (seg-head build), **P1-04** (frozen-embedding probe),
**P1-09** (continued-pretraining).

## Step P1-03 — 2026-07-12: Stage-1 per-position 8-class segmentation head (Linear + optional CRF)

**Inputs.** The 8 normative class identifiers (ADR-0004 D1), single-sourced from
`tbox_finder.labels.CLASS_ORDER`; a random `L×d_model` hidden-state fixture (`torch.randn`, no backbone
load needed). No in-repo segmentation head existed — it had to be built (PRD §6/§8/§10.1).

**Process.** Built `SegmentationHead` (`src/tbox_finder/models/seg_head.py::SegmentationHead`): a
per-position `Linear(input_dim → 8)` projection over the Caduceus-PS hidden states, with an **optional**
in-repo `LinearChainCRF` transition layer for boundary coherence (`use_crf` knob so P2 can ablate
Linear-only vs Linear+CRF, ADR-0004). The CRF is a standard **batch-first linear-chain CRF** —
`torch.logsumexp` forward algorithm for the partition function, Viterbi for decoding — implemented
in-repo with **no external `torchcrf`/`pytorch-crf` dependency** (the P1-03 note's default, so no env
amendment). The head is **8-class by construction** (`NUM_CLASSES = len(CLASS_ORDER)`); the input width
default `CADUCEUS_PS_HIDDEN_DIM = 2*d_model = 512` is single-sourced from `caduceus_backbone.D_MODEL`
(the RC-concatenated hidden width) so the two cannot drift. `decode()` returns per-sequence best paths
(CRF Viterbi, else per-position argmax); `loss()` is CRF NLL, else masked cross-entropy
(`F.cross_entropy` K-dim form + `ignore_index`).

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/models/seg_head.py` (head + CRF; torch imported at module top — the
submodule *is* a torch model, but the `models` package `__init__` stays lazy so the package still
imports bare); `tests/unit/test_seg_head.py` (24 cases: 3 bare-tier — the ADR-0004 class-order/index +
`2*d_model` contract, run in CI — + a 21-test torch tier under `tbox-ml-dna`). **No DVC artifact**
(library + tests; git-tracked). **No `.smk`/data change** (DAG lint unaffected).

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** — logits shape `(B,L,8)` (Linear-only and CRF); the CRF `viterbi_decode`
returns a length-L valid class sequence (every index in `[0,8)`); class-index↔identifier mapping matches
the ADR-0004 literal. Torch tier **21 passed** under `tbox-ml-dna` (CPU — no GPU needed); bare CI tier
**3 passed / 18 skipped** (torch tier skips cleanly, no collection error); full unit suite **465 passed /
25 skipped** bare (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean. Behavioral gates that *bite*: the
CRF reduces to per-position argmax when its transitions vanish, and a stay-in-tag-0 transition bias
overrides the emission argmax (the transition layer is proven load-bearing); partition ≥ gold-path score
⇒ NLL ≥ 0 per sequence; masked decode truncates to the mask lengths.

**Decisions.** In-repo CRF chosen over a third-party dep to keep the step self-contained (no
`envs/ml.yml` amendment / §3.1 approval). Torch lives at the seg_head module top (idiomatic `nn.Module`),
so the bare-CI test tier guards with `skipif(not torch)` and the load-bearing class-order invariant is
asserted torch-free from `tbox_finder.labels` — the head never redeclares the vocabulary, so a logit
index always maps to the ADR-0004 identifier at that position. The RC-combination that feeds this head
stays directionality-preserving upstream (P1-02/P1-04, §6 strand-resolver); this head is
combination-agnostic (it consumes an `(B,L,2·d_model)` hidden state). context7 §3.3: `/pytorch/pytorch`
consulted for `F.cross_entropy` K-dim `(N,C,d)` + `ignore_index` and `nn.Parameter` registration; the
CRF forward/Viterbi correctness is verified by the executed test under the installed torch 2.7.1
(P1-01/P1-02 installed-library-cross-check precedent). Cross-link **ADR-0004 D1**.

**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-03-seg-head` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-03 close); feeds **P1-04** (backbone+head assembly with the directionality-preserving
RC-combination knob) and **P1-09** (continued-pretraining consumes the same head).

## Step P1-04 — 2026-07-12: Assemble the Stage-1 segmenter (backbone → RC-combine → seg head) with a directionality-preserving RC-combination knob

**Inputs.** The P1-02 Caduceus-PS backbone (`D_MODEL=256`, `_hidden_states → (B,L,2·d_model)=512`); the
P1-03 `SegmentationHead` (`input_dim=CADUCEUS_PS_HIDDEN_DIM=512`); the 8-class `tbox_finder.labels.CLASS_ORDER`
(Specifier=2, Antiterminator_Tbox_seq=5); a synthetic asymmetric hidden-state fixture + its forward↔RC
channel-half swap (no backbone forward — CPU-testable).

**Process.** Built `src/tbox_finder/models/stage1_segmenter.py::Stage1Segmenter` (an `nn.Module` wiring
backbone → `RCCombine` → `SegmentationHead`) and `RCCombine` — the `rc_combine` knob restricted to
**directionality-preserving (non-averaged)** forms (PRD §6/§10.1; ADR-0002): `concat` (**default**;
identity — feed the full `(fwd‖rc)` 512-d state to the head, injective ⇒ order-preserving) and `gate` (a
learned directional per-channel gate `g⊙fwd+(1-g)⊙rc → d_model`, `g=sigmoid(logit)` init nonzero so
`g≠0.5` ⇒ asymmetric by construction). An order-destroying **mean** (`0.5·(fwd+rc)`) is **rejected** at
construction and is **not the default**. The mode contract (`ALLOWED_RC_COMBINE={concat,gate}`,
`normalize_rc_combine`, `is_directionality_preserving`) is single-sourced from a **torch-free**
`src/tbox_finder/models/rc_combine.py` so bare CI locks it; the segmenter re-exports it. Added the
`rc_combine` group to `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml` (declarative `mode: concat`; the allowed set +
mean-rejection are frozen in code, not config). The backbone forward (`forward(input_ids=…)`, needs CUDA,
ADR-0002 A2 C2) is deferred to the P1-07 fine-tune smoke; the CPU surface is `logits_from_hidden` /
`decode_from_hidden` / `loss_from_hidden`.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/models/{stage1_segmenter,rc_combine}.py` + `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml`
(+`rc_combine` group) + `tests/unit/test_rc_combination.py` (5 bare-tier + 20 torch-tier). No DVC artifact
(library + tests + config; git-tracked). No ADR amendment (implements the ADR-0002 / PRD §6/§10.1 pinned
constraint; no new decision/threshold).

**Validation.** Unit green. Torch tier **25 passed** under `tbox-ml-dna` (CPU); bare CI tier **5 passed /
20 skipped** (torch tier skips cleanly — the mode contract is locked torch-free); full bare unit suite **no
regression** (0 failures; the new file contributes exactly +5 passed / +20 skipped vs `main`); ruff 0.15.15
+ black 25.11.0 clean; `snakemake --lint` good + `-n` resolves (no `.smk` change, DAG unaffected). The
directionality gate **bites**: the allowed `concat`/`gate` combines are **NOT** invariant under the
forward↔RC channel-half swap (element order survives), whereas a reference symmetric **mean IS** invariant
(order collapses); end-to-end, a `concat` segmenter with a controlled head decodes a **strand-dependent**
element order (`[2,2,0,5,5,0]` = Specifier→Antiterminator for the forward channels vs all-background for the
swapped input), while a mean-combined reference decodes **identically** for a window and its opposite strand
— the exact collapse the §6 strand-resolver cannot tolerate.

**Decisions.** The RC-combination is the load-bearing link Stage-1 → the §6 strand-resolver: orientation is
derived from predicted element order, so an order-averaging combine would defeat it — hence `mean` is not a
selectable mode (not merely a non-default), and the allowed non-averaged set is frozen in code so no config
can weaken it. `concat` is the default because Caduceus-PS's `2·d_model` hidden state already carries the
`(fwd‖rc)` channels and feeding them whole is injective (P1-03's head `input_dim=512` is exactly this path);
`gate` is the second non-averaged form for the P2 RC-combination ablation (PRD §11 Sweeps). The
`swap_strand_channels` fwd/RC half-swap is the channel-space action of "read the opposite strand" — the
minimal symmetry under which an order-destroying average is invariant and a directionality-preserving form
is not. context7 §3.3: `/pytorch/pytorch` consulted for `torch.chunk`/`torch.split`/`torch.cat` + the GLU
split-half+`sigmoid` gating idiom + `nn.Parameter`/`torch.sigmoid`; correctness verified by the executed
test under installed torch 2.7.1+cu128 (P1-01/02/03 installed-library-cross-check precedent). Cross-link
**ADR-0002** (RC-combination constraint / D7) and **PRD §6/§10.1**.

**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-04-segmenter` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-04 close); feeds **P1-07** (the binding segmentation smoke fine-tune consumes this
segmenter) and **P1-05/P1-08** (the frozen-embedding probe uses the backbone-free `logits_from_hidden` path).

## Step P1-05 — 2026-07-12: Transfer go/no-go part (i) — frozen-embedding linear-separability pre-filter (cheap, non-binding)
**Inputs.** Frozen Caduceus-PS embeddings (7.73M params, pinned revision `d89eeb85…`, human-reference
pretrained) of **8,715 held-out (leave-clade-out) bacterial T-box windows** (positives — `split_assignments.parquet`
`nested_role == heldout` & `source == corpus`; window DNA from `class_I/II.fa`) vs **2,000 GC-matched prokaryotic
background windows** (negatives — `decoys_v0.parquet` pool `gc_background`; §9.1 class 1). **Process.** Two-stage,
no env change: (A) extract mean-pooled 512-d frozen embeddings on the **laptop GPU** (RTX 4060 sm_89, `tbox-ml-dna`,
~9 min at ~21 windows/s batch=1) → npz cache; (B) fit a `StandardScaler`+`LogisticRegression(class_weight=balanced,
C=1)` probe under `tbox-data` on a **homology-safe split** (`GroupShuffleSplit` by `cluster_id` — §8.2: no sequence
cluster spans the boundary), scored on the held-out probe split with a 2,000-draw seeded AUROC bootstrap CI. Entry
`src/tbox_finder/probes/frozen_linear_probe.py::run_prefilter` (`--stage extract|probe|all`). **Outputs.**
`reports/p1/prefilter_separability.json` (git-tracked, `measured=true`): **AUROC 0.9921** (95% CI 0.9887–0.9950),
**balanced-accuracy 0.9597** on a 2,963-pos / 500-neg held-out probe split; inline provenance (git_sha, both env-lock
hashes, seed 42, input SHA-256s). The 22 MB embeddings npz is a gitignored **regenerable** intermediate (not
DVC-tracked; the report hashes it), per the kernel_smoke precedent.
**Validation.** ADR-0002 D7 part-(i) *above-chance* pre-filter → **PASS** (AUROC-CI-lower 0.989 ≫ chance 0.5);
**non-binding** (a low pre-filter can coexist with a passing full fine-tune — the *binding* gate is the P1-07
per-nucleotide segmentation smoke). Leakage: `cluster_spans_boundary=false`, `n_shared_clusters=0`; positives
held-out-fold only. Tests: **31 passed** (stdlib + sklearn tiers — verdict-bites, validator-bites incl. §8.2
fail-closed, committed-report validity, homology-safe partition); the torch-tier extraction test **passed under
`tbox-ml-dna` on the GPU** (skips in CI); **27 pass bare**. ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean.
**Decisions.** (1) Grouped the probe split by **`cluster_id`, not `resolved_order`**: ~41 clusters span >1 order
and carry ~20% of the held-out positives, so an order-grouped split leaks clusters while dropping them is too lossy
— the §8.2 homology unit (sequence cluster) is load-bearing, and order-spanning across *distinct* clusters is not
homology leakage (reported as informational, `order_spans_boundary=true`). (2) **Two-stage across two envs**
(`tbox-ml-dna` torch + `tbox-data` sklearn) because no single committed env carries both → avoids an ADR-0002 D8
env bump/re-lock. (3) The verdict operationalization (PASS iff AUROC bootstrap-CI-lower > 0.5) is an **implementer
operationalization** of the ADR's qualitative *above-chance* criterion, not a new ADR-pinned number (P0-31 precedent);
ADR-0002 pins no numeric pre-filter floor. context7 §3.3: `/websites/scikit-learn_stable` consulted for
`LogisticRegression`/`GroupShuffleSplit`/`StandardScaler` pipeline; the transformers embedding path reuses the
P1-02-verified `caduceus_backbone` loader (pinned-revision, frozen). Cross-link **ADR-0002 D7**, **PRD §10.1 / §18.2**.
**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-05-frozen-linear-probe` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-05 close); feeds **P1-07** (advisory input to the binding go/no-go adjudication) and
**P1-08** (Stage-1 smoke reproducibility).

## Step P1-06 — 2026-07-12: Held-out prokaryotic per-nt 8-class segmentation smoke set (ADR-0004/0005 labels)
**Inputs.** The committed **git-tracked** split table (`data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet`, PRD §9.2);
the DVC-tracked corpus (`master_clean_v0.parquet`) + P0 labels (`labels_v0.parquet`); the P0 label-derivation
function (`tbox_finder.labels.derive_label_codes`, ADR-0004 D1). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/data/seg_smoke.py::build_smoke_set`
(rule `workflow/rules/backbones.smk::p1_seg_smoke_set`, LOCAL, `tbox-data`) selects held-out loci with the **verbatim
P1-05 predicate** `nested_role=="heldout" & source=="corpus"` (no "prokaryotic" predicate — the corpus is all-bacterial,
no domain column exists), reuses the committed per-nt `label_string` (re-derived via `derive_label_codes` on the same
coords as a **fail-loud consistency guard**, §10.3 — no biology re-derived), and writes one row per locus + a padded
per-nt label matrix. A deterministic, `record_id`-sorted, **rarest-class-first coverage-greedy** selection guarantees
all 8 classes + ≥1 class-II locus (so the class-I-only-Terminator invariant is non-vacuous). **Outputs.**
`data/interim/p1_seg_smoke/windows.parquet` (300 loci; DVC) + `labels.npy` (**(300, 547) int16, pad = −100** = the
ADR-0005 seg-head ignore-index, `allow_pickle=False`; DVC) + `windows.provenance.json` (git) +
`data/processed/audits/seg_smoke_report.json` (git; digest `3d6f77f0…`, per-class coverage, `positives_heldout_only=true`,
239 clusters / 26 orders); the golden CI slice `tests/fixtures/p1_seg_smoke/heldout_slice.csv` (24 real held-out loci
+ coords, CI-reproducible without the DVC tables) + `expected.sha256` (`3c495c04…`).
**Validation.** **Golden PASS** — the smoke digest reproduces `expected.sha256` from a **stdlib-only** CSV re-hash
(bare CI) *and* the pandas rebuild; digests are **PYTHONHASHSEED-independent** (verified seeds 0/1/random/7).
**Coverage PASS** — all 8 classes present; **0** ADR-0004 violations (`terminator_on_class_ii=0`,
`specifier_carveout_violations=0`, `antiterm_term_violations=0`). **Leakage PASS (§8.2)** — 100% of loci are
held-out-fold; the full-corpus `tests/ml/test_no_leakage.py` re-run stays green (P1-06 only *selects* from the
unchanged split table — it perturbs no cluster/taxon membership). Tests: `test_seg_smoke_leakage.py` (20: predicate,
deterministic/sorted selection, class coverage, order-independent digest, +6 fail-closed bite tests incl.
non-held-out / class-II-Terminator / length-mismatch / label-drift, numpy pad tier, committed-fixture 100%-held-out
tier) + `test_seg_smoke_set.py` (7: stdlib + pandas golden, label-are-P0-derivation, coverage/conventions). **Full
suite 569 passed / 49 skipped** (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean; `snakemake --lint` good + rule
`-n` resolves.
**Decisions.** (1) **CSV fixture** (not parquet): pandas reads it without pyarrow, human-diffable, matches the
`ingest_sample` reuse-a-committed-slice precedent; the split table is git-tracked but master/labels are DVC → the
golden must be self-contained (a digest-critical column set is force-`str` on reload so dtype inference can't alter a
hashed token). (2) **Reuse the committed P0 `label_string`, cross-checked against `derive_label_codes`** — the most
literal "no re-derivation" reading, with drift caught loudly. (3) **Padded 2-D int16 matrix, pad = −100** (not a
ragged/object array): deterministic, hashable, pickle-free (the P1-05 pickle-surface lesson), and directly the
seg-head cross-entropy `ignore_index`. (4) **Smoke digest hashes `(record_id, FASTA_sequence, label_string)`**, sorted
by `record_id` — reuses the `ingest.record_hash`/`records_digest` contract (parquet-byte-independent, the labels_golden
precedent); the golden is a regression on the committed **fixture** slice, not the full 300-locus artifact (its
provenance carries the production digest). context7 §3.3: n/a (reuses in-repo `labels`/`ingest`/`provenance`; pandas/
numpy only for IO). Cross-link **ADR-0004 D1**, **ADR-0005**, **PRD §8/§9.2**.
**Commit.** atomic commit on `p1-06-seg-smoke`; feeds **P1-07** (the BINDING per-nt segmentation smoke fine-tune
consumes this set) + **P1-09** (continued-pretraining).
